structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Fan Speed and SAT Control in Heating Mode
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit (FCU) with heating coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Heating, fan speed and SAT setpoint are reset based on heating-loop signal. The heating coil is modulated to maintain discharge temperature at SATsp.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Heating and there is a heating coil.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone heating setpoint. The heating coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. Cooling coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If there is a heating coil, when Zone State is Heating: 1. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed. 2. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed. 3. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT. 4. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone heating setpoint. 5. The heating coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Cooling coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Fan Speed and SAT Control in Deadband Mode
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Deadband, fan speed is set to DeadbandSpeed. If DeadbandSpeed is zero, the fan is shut off. Both heating and cooling coils are OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: FANsp shall be DeadbandSpeed. If DeadbandSpeed is zero, shut the fan off. Cooling coil OFF. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When Zone State is Deadband: 1. FANsp shall be DeadbandSpeed. If DeadbandSpeed is zero, shut the fan off. 2. Cooling coil OFF 3. Heating coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Filter Pressure Drop Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm when filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed. The alarm limit varies with fan speed as DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate a Level 4 alarm. The alarm limit shall vary with fan speed as DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed: Level 4. The alarm limit shall vary with fan speed as follows: DPx = DP100 (x)^1.4

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Too Many Changes in Operating State
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the number of changes in Operating State during the previous 60 minutes exceeds the maximum (default 7), a fault condition is detected.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: ΔOS > ΔOS MAX (default 7) for AlarmDelay minutes (default 30).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report a Level 3 alarm with description 'Too many changes in Operating State' and possible diagnosis 'Unstable control due to poorly tuned loop or mechanical problem'.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #1 Equation ΔOS > ΔOS MAX Applies to OS #1 – #4 Description Too many changes in Operating State Possible Diagnosis Unstable control due to poorly tuned loop or mechanical problem

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Temperature Drop Across Inactive Cooling Coil
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit (FCU) with cooling coil and RAT sensor
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT is significantly lower than RAT when both heating and cooling valves are closed, a fault condition is detected.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: SAT AVG – RAT ≥ √(ƐRAT^2 + ƐSAT^2) + ΔTSF for AlarmDelay minutes (default 30) in OS#2 (cooling only FCU with RAT).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report a Level 3 alarm with description 'Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil' and possible diagnoses including RAT sensor error, SAT sensor error, cooling coil valve stuck open or leaking, DX cooling stuck on.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#4 Equation SAT AVG – RAT ≥ √ƐRAT2+ ƐSAT2 + ΔTSF Applies to OS #2 (cooling only FCU with RAT) Description Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error, SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck open or leaking, DX cooling stuck on

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Temperature Rise Across Inactive Heating Coil
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit (FCU) with heating coil and RAT sensor
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT is significantly higher than RAT when both heating and cooling valves are closed, a fault condition is detected.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: SAT AVG - RAT ≤ √(ƐRAT^2 + ƐSAT^2) + ΔTSF for AlarmDelay minutes (default 30) in OS#2 (heating only FCU with RAT).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report a Level 3 alarm with description 'Temperature rise across inactive heating coil' and possible diagnoses including RAT sensor error, SAT sensor error, heating coil valve stuck open or leaking, gas or electric heat stuck on.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#5 Equation SAT AVG - RAT ≤ √ƐRAT2+ ƐSAT2 + ΔTSF Applies to OS #2 (heating only FCU with RAT) Description Temperature rise across inactive heating coil Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error, SAT sensor error, Heating coil valve stuck open or leaking, Gas or electric heat stuck on

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Evaluation Suspension on Mode Change
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended for a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode of any Zone Group served by the FCU.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode or Cooldown Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the FCU.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suspend evaluation of fault conditions for ModeDelay minutes (default 30).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: ... b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode or Cooldown Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the FCU.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Points Required
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU: SAT, RAT (if present), SATsp, HC, CC, and FS.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Ensure SAT, RAT (if present), SATsp, HC, CC, and FS are available.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU: a. SAT = supply air temperature b. RAT = return air temperature (if present) c. SATsp = supply air temperature setpoint d. HC = heating-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ HC ≤ 100% e. CC = cooling-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ CC ≤ 100% f. FS = fan-speed command; 0% ≤ FS ≤ 100%

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Calculated Values
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of SAT, RAT (if fitted), and ΔOS shall be continuously calculated.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate 5-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of SAT avg, RAT avg (if fitted), and ΔOS.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The following values must be continuously calculated by the AFDD routines for each FCU: a. Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently. 1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature 2. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature (if fitted) 3. ΔOS = number of changes in OS during the previous 60 minutes (moving window)

structured_payload_candidate.summary: Internal variables for AFDD include ΔTSF, ƐSAT, ƐRAT, ΔOSmax, ModeDelay, AlarmDelay, and TestModeDelay, with default values as shown in Table 5.20.6.5.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Applicable Fault Conditions per OS
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The set of applicable fault conditions depends on the OS of the FCU.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: FCU is in a specific OS.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: In OS#1 (Heating): evaluate FC#1 and FC#2. In OS#2 (Deadband): evaluate FC#5 (heating only FCU) or FC#4 (cooling only FCU). In OS#3 (Cooling): evaluate FC#1 and FC#3. In OS#4 (other): evaluate FC#1.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. In OS#1 (Heating), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS 2. FC#2: SAT too low in full heating b. In OS#2 (Deadband), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#5: Temperature drop across inactive heating coil (heating only FCU 2. FC#4: Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil (cooling only FCU c. In OS#3 (Cooling), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS 2. FC#3: SAT too high in full cooling d. In OS#4 (other), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS
